revision.comment: 新規作成,三重県亀山市の神社,「ウィキペディアタウン亀山」において作成
/* top */
/* 参考文献 */
『鈴鹿郡誌』とあるのは『鈴鹿郡郷土誌』のことではなかろうか、『鈴鹿郡郷土誌』には△1ページがあり、そのページにはそれらしい記述がある…
境内節や神事節の追加
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=忍山神社 |所在地=[[三重県]][[亀山市]]野村四丁目4-65 |祭神=[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]
[[天照皇大神]]
[[天児屋根命]] |画像=[[ファイル:Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (2).jpg|280px]] |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 51|緯度秒 = 17.19|N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 136|経度分 = 26|経度秒 = 13.28|E(東経)及びW(西経) =E |社格=[[式内社]] |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=10月14日 |神事=[[傘鉾]]巡行 |地図= }} '''忍山神社'''（おしやまじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[亀山市]]野村四丁目4-65にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]（さるたひこのみこと）『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25 * [[天照大神]]（あまてらすおおみかみ） ; 合祀祭神 * [[天児屋命]]（あめのこやねのみこと）など == 歴史 == [[ファイル:Ototachibanahime.png|thumb|170px|弟橘姫命]] 当地は、[[弟橘媛|弟橘姫命]]の生誕の地である{{Sfn|鈴鹿郡教育会|1981|p=1}}。かつては、愛宕山（亀山市野村町）に所在したというが、[[奈良時代]]に至って[[神宮寺]]を併置するに至った『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178。 [[平安時代]]には[[平重盛]]によって社殿が造営され、社勢を誇った{{R|亀山地方}}。文永11年（1274年）の[[文永の役]]により、社殿はすべて失われたため、白木山に避難し、布気神社の境内に仮宮をなした{{R|亀山地方}}。しかし、布気神社はそのまま、忍山神社にとってかわられることになる{{R|亀山地方}}。 [[室町時代]]の文明5年（1473年）には兵火で社殿を焼失し、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の永正3年（1506年）には再び火災で神殿などを焼失した。 1908年（明治41年）には野村にあるすべての村社と無格社を合祀した。1917年（大正6年）10月には鎮座2000年祭を斎行した。 == 境内 == * 本殿 * 社殿 * 社務所 * 手水舎 * 南参道 鳥居 * 東参道 鳥居 Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|本殿 Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|社殿の内部 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|社務所 Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|南参道の鳥居 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|東参道の鳥居 Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|社叢 == 神事 == * 傘鉾 - 祭神のひとつである[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]の荒魂を鎮めるために古くからおこなわれていた神事であるパンフレット「忍山神社」。毎年10月14日の例祭日、傘鉾と呼ばれる巨大な傘状の[[山車]]が町内を巡行する[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市。3本の傘鉾は毎年[[氏子]]らによって新調され、赤・青・白・黄・緑の五色の色紙が貼られて仕上げられる。もとは能牟良神社（のむらじんじゃ）の祭礼であり、忍山神社が能牟良神社を合祀したことで忍山神社の祭礼となった。1952年（昭和27年）12月には亀山市無形民俗文化財に指定された。 == 交通アクセス == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[関西本線]][[亀山駅 (三重県)|亀山駅]]から徒歩25分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年 * 亀山市教育委員会『ヤマトタケルとオトタチバナヒメ物語』亀山市教育委員会、2023年 * {{Cite book|和書 |title=鈴鹿郡郷土誌 復刻版 |publisher=東天社 |year=1981 |ref = harv|isbn = 4-924599-06-9 |editor = 鈴鹿郡教育会}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oshiyama-jinja}} * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしやましんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:亀山市の歴史]] [[Category:亀山市の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:弟橘媛]]

revision.comment: 起源説を追加
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=忍山神社 |所在地=[[三重県]][[亀山市]]野村四丁目4-65 |祭神=[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]
[[天照皇大神]]
[[天児屋根命]] |画像=[[ファイル:Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (2).jpg|280px]] |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 51|緯度秒 = 17.19|N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 136|経度分 = 26|経度秒 = 13.28|E(東経)及びW(西経) =E |社格=[[式内社]] |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=10月14日 |神事=[[傘鉾]]巡行 |地図= }} '''忍山神社'''（おしやまじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[亀山市]]野村四丁目4-65にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]（さるたひこのみこと）『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25 * [[天照大神]]（あまてらすおおみかみ） ; 合祀祭神 * [[天児屋命]]（あめのこやねのみこと）など == 歴史 == [[ファイル:Ototachibanahime.png|thumb|170px|弟橘姫命]] 伝承によれば、紀元前91年（[[崇神天皇]]7年)に[[物部氏]]の祖である[[伊香色雄]]が勅命を受けて[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]を鎮座し、伊香我色の子である[[大水口宿禰]]の子孫が明治時代に入るまで80代にわたって神主を務めた。また、[[垂仁天皇]]の皇女・[[倭姫命]]が忍山に遷幸した際に半年滞在した跡をかしこみ[[天照大神]]を奉った。 また、当地は、[[弟橘媛|弟橘姫命]]の生誕の地として知られる{{Sfn|鈴鹿郡教育会|1981|p=1}}。弟橘姫命は神主・忍山宿祢の長女で、[[景行天皇]]の王子[[ヤマトタケル]]の妻。ヤマトタケルが東征にあたって当地に立ち寄った際にその妻となり、東征に同道して、相模の海が海神の怒りに触れて嵐となった際に入水して鎮めたという。 かつては、愛宕山（亀山市野村町）に所在したというが、[[奈良時代]]に至って[[神宮寺]]を併置するに至った『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178。 [[平安時代]]には[[平重盛]]によって社殿が造営され、社勢を誇った{{R|亀山地方}}。文永11年（1274年）の[[文永の役]]により、社殿はすべて失われたため、白木山に避難し、布気神社の境内に仮宮をなした{{R|亀山地方}}。しかし、布気神社はそのまま、忍山神社にとってかわられることになる{{R|亀山地方}}。 [[室町時代]]の文明5年（1473年）には兵火で社殿を焼失し、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の永正3年（1506年）には再び火災で神殿などを焼失した。 1908年（明治41年）には野村にあるすべての村社と無格社の計20社を合祀した。1917年（大正6年）10月には鎮座2000年祭を斎行した。野村、野村住宅、和賀地区の氏神である{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher =三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}。 == 境内 == * 本殿 * 社殿 * 社務所 * 手水舎 * 南参道 鳥居 * 東参道 鳥居 Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|本殿 Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|社殿の内部 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|社務所 Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|南参道の鳥居 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|東参道の鳥居 Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|社叢 == 神事 == * 傘鉾 - 祭神の一柱である[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]の荒魂を鎮めるために古くからおこなわれていた神事であるパンフレット「忍山神社」。毎年10月14日の例祭日、傘鉾と呼ばれる巨大な傘状の[[山車]]が町内を巡行する[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市。3本の傘鉾は毎年[[氏子]]らによって新調され、赤・青・白・黄・緑の五色の色紙が貼られて仕上げられる。もとは能牟良神社（のむらじんじゃ）の祭礼であり、忍山神社が能牟良神社を合祀したことで忍山神社の祭礼となった。1952年（昭和27年）12月には亀山市無形民俗文化財に指定された。 == 交通アクセス == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[関西本線]][[亀山駅 (三重県)|亀山駅]]から徒歩25分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年 * 亀山市教育委員会『ヤマトタケルとオトタチバナヒメ物語』亀山市教育委員会、2023年 * {{Cite book|和書 |title=鈴鹿郡郷土誌 復刻版 |publisher=東天社 |year=1981 |ref = harv|isbn = 4-924599-06-9 |editor = 鈴鹿郡教育会}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oshiyama-jinja}} * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしやましんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:亀山市の歴史]] [[Category:亀山市の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:弟橘媛]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=忍山神社 |所在地=[[三重県]][[亀山市]]野村四丁目4-65 |祭神=[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]
[[天照皇大神]]
[[天児屋根命]] |画像=[[ファイル:Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (2).jpg|280px]] |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 51|緯度秒 = 17.19|N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 136|経度分 = 26|経度秒 = 13.28|E(東経)及びW(西経) =E |社格=[[式内社]] |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=10月14日 |神事=[[傘鉾]]巡行 |地図= }} '''忍山神社'''（おしやまじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[亀山市]]野村四丁目4-65にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]（さるたひこのみこと）『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25 * [[天照大神]]（あまてらすおおみかみ） ; 合祀祭神 * [[天児屋命]]（あめのこやねのみこと）など == 歴史 == === 古代 === [[ファイル:Ototachibanahime.png|thumb|170px|弟橘姫命]] 伝承によれば、紀元前91年（[[崇神天皇]]7年)に[[物部氏]]の祖である[[伊香色雄]]が勅命を受けて[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]を鎮座し、伊香我色の子である[[大水口宿禰]]の子孫が明治時代に入るまで80代にわたって神主を務めた。また、[[垂仁天皇]]の皇女・[[倭姫命]]が忍山に遷幸した際に半年滞在した跡をかしこみ[[天照大神]]を奉った。 また、当地は、[[弟橘媛|弟橘姫命]]の生誕の地として知られる{{Sfn|鈴鹿郡教育会|1981|p=1}}。弟橘姫命は神主・忍山宿祢の長女で、[[景行天皇]]の王子[[ヤマトタケル]]の妻。ヤマトタケルが東征にあたって当地に立ち寄った際にその妻となり、東征に同道して、相模の海が海神の怒りに触れて嵐となった際に入水して鎮めたという。 かつては、愛宕山（亀山市野村町）に所在したというが、[[奈良時代]]に至って[[神宮寺]]を併置するに至った『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178。 === 中世・近世 === [[File:Kameyama-jinja Okubo House Gate ac.jpg|thumb|left|大久保神官家棟門]] [[平安時代]]には[[平重盛]]によって社殿が造営され、社勢を誇った{{R|亀山地方}}。文永11年（1274年）の[[文永の役]]により、社殿はすべて失われたため、白木山に避難し、布気神社の境内に仮宮をなした{{R|亀山地方}}。しかし、布気神社はそのまま、忍山神社にとってかわられることになる{{R|亀山地方}}。 [[室町時代]]の文明5年（1473年）には兵火で社殿を焼失し、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の永正3年（1506年）には再び火災で神殿などを焼失した。[[江戸時代]]には忍山神社の神官である大久保但馬守の邸宅が竣工し、後に[[亀山神社 (亀山市)|亀山神社]]に移築されると、1955年（昭和30年）2月19日には「大久保神官家棟門」として亀山市指定文化財に指定されている[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市。 === 近代・現代 === 1908年（明治41年）には野村にあるすべての村社と無格社の計20社を合祀した。1917年（大正6年）10月には鎮座2000年祭を斎行した。野村、野村住宅、和賀地区の氏神である{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher =三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}。 == 境内 == * 本殿 * 社殿 * 社務所 * 手水舎 * 南参道 鳥居 * 東参道 鳥居 Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|本殿 Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|社殿の内部 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|社務所 Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|南参道の鳥居 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|東参道の鳥居 Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|社叢 == 神事 == * 傘鉾 - 祭神の一柱である[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]の荒魂を鎮めるために古くからおこなわれていた神事であるパンフレット「忍山神社」。毎年10月14日の例祭日、傘鉾と呼ばれる巨大な傘状の[[山車]]が町内を巡行する[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市。3本の傘鉾は毎年[[氏子]]らによって新調され、赤・青・白・黄・緑の五色の色紙が貼られて仕上げられる。もとは能牟良神社（のむらじんじゃ）の祭礼であり、忍山神社が能牟良神社を合祀したことで忍山神社の祭礼となった。1952年（昭和27年）12月には亀山市無形民俗文化財に指定された。 == 交通アクセス == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[関西本線]][[亀山駅 (三重県)|亀山駅]]から徒歩25分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年 * 亀山市教育委員会『ヤマトタケルとオトタチバナヒメ物語』亀山市教育委員会、2023年 * {{Cite book|和書 |title=鈴鹿郡郷土誌 復刻版 |publisher=東天社 |year=1981 |ref = harv|isbn = 4-924599-06-9 |editor = 鈴鹿郡教育会}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oshiyama-jinja}} * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしやましんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:亀山市の歴史]] [[Category:亀山市の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:弟橘媛]]

revision.comment: 神事と植生を加筆。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=忍山神社 |所在地=[[三重県]][[亀山市]]野村四丁目4-65 |祭神=[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]

|神事=[[傘鉾]]巡行 |地図= }} '''忍山神社'''（おしやまじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[亀山市]]野村四丁目4-65にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]。 == 祭神 == ; 主祭神 * [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]（さるたひこのみこと）『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25 * [[天照大神]]（あまてらすおおみかみ） ; 合祀祭神 * [[天児屋命]]（あめのこやねのみこと）など == 歴史 == === 古代 === [[ファイル:Ototachibanahime.png|thumb|170px|弟橘姫命]] 伝承によれば、紀元前91年（[[崇神天皇]]7年)に[[物部氏]]の祖である[[伊香色雄]]が勅命を受けて[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]を鎮座し、伊香我色の子である[[大水口宿禰]]の子孫が明治時代に入るまで80代にわたって神主を務めた。また、[[垂仁天皇]]の皇女・[[倭姫命]]が忍山に遷幸した際に半年滞在した跡をかしこみ[[天照大神]]を奉った。 また、当地は、[[弟橘媛|弟橘姫命]]の生誕の地として知られる{{Sfn|鈴鹿郡教育会|1981|p=1}}。弟橘姫命は神主・忍山宿祢の長女で、[[景行天皇]]の王子[[ヤマトタケル]]の妻。ヤマトタケルが東征にあたって当地に立ち寄った際にその妻となり、東征に同道して、相模の海が海神の怒りに触れて嵐となった際に入水して鎮めたという。 かつては、愛宕山（亀山市野村町）に所在したというが、[[奈良時代]]に至って[[神宮寺]]を併置するに至った『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178。 === 中世・近世 === [[File:Kameyama-jinja Okubo House Gate ac.jpg|thumb|left|大久保神官家棟門]] [[平安時代]]には[[平重盛]]によって社殿が造営され、社勢を誇った{{R|亀山地方}}。文永11年（1274年）の[[文永の役]]により、社殿はすべて失われたため、白木山に避難し、布気神社の境内に仮宮をなした{{R|亀山地方}}。しかし、布気神社はそのまま、忍山神社にとってかわられることになる{{R|亀山地方}}。 [[室町時代]]の文明5年（1473年）には兵火で社殿を焼失し、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の永正3年（1506年）には再び火災で神殿などを焼失した。[[江戸時代]]には忍山神社の神官である大久保但馬守の邸宅が竣工し、後に[[亀山神社 (亀山市)|亀山神社]]に移築されると、1955年（昭和30年）2月19日には「大久保神官家棟門」として亀山市指定文化財に指定されている[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市。 === 近代・現代 === 1908年（明治41年）には野村にあるすべての村社と無格社の計20社を合祀した。1917年（大正6年）10月には鎮座2000年祭を斎行した。野村、野村住宅、和賀地区の氏神である{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher =三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}。 == 境内 == === 建造物 === * 本殿 * 社殿 * 社務所 * 手水舎 * 南参道 鳥居 * 東参道 鳥居 Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|本殿 Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|社殿の内部 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|社務所 Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|南参道の鳥居 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|東参道の鳥居 Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|社叢 === 植生 === 亀山市の巨樹・古木・注目すべき木として数えられる11本のうち、以下の２本が忍山神社境内にある{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher =亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}。 1本は[[モチノキ]]科[[クロガネモチ]]の巨木で、本殿右側にあり、幹周り263センチメートル、樹高11.5メートル。根が盛り上がり[[ヒサカキ]]を巻き込んで生育する。 もう１本はイヌガヤ科[[イヌガヤ]]の古木で、南側の境内の入口にあり、幹周り43センチメートル。かつてイヌガヤの実から油を採取し、神前の灯火に用いたという説がある。 == 神事 == 秋の例祭の神事に、1952年（昭和27年）12月には亀山市無形民俗文化財に指定された'''「傘鉾」'''巡行がある。祭神の一柱である[[スサノオ|須佐之男命]]の荒魂を鎮めるために古くからおこなわれていた神事でパンフレット「忍山神社」、毎年10月14日の例祭日、傘鉾と呼ばれる巨大な傘状の[[山車]]が、太鼓を打ち鳴らしながら隊列を組み、町内を巡行する[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市。 巡行の始点は旧[[東海道]]の京口坂で、最初西進し、[[野村一里塚]]で折り返して北に向かい、野村町を巡って終点が'''忍山神社'''である。3本の傘鉾は毎年[[氏子]]らによって新調され、16等分に割った真竹に赤・青・白・黄・緑の五色の色紙が貼られて仕上げられる{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/jirei/bunrui10/data10-4/index10_4_2.htm |title=亀山市史 民俗編 祭礼・芸能（四）傘鉾の制作 |publisher =亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}。この色紙は巡行の道中で氏子らがちぎりとるもので、家内安全や災難退散などの御利益があるので持ち帰り各家の神棚などに献上される{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/jirei/bunrui10/data10-4/index10_4.htm |title=亀山市史 民俗編 祭礼・芸能（四）傘鉾 |publisher =亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/jirei/bunrui10/data10-4/index10_4_1.htm |title=亀山市史 民俗編 祭礼・芸能（四）傘鉾行事次第 |publisher =亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}。古くは傘鉾の紙は色紙でなく、昭和前期には村内安全や五穀豊穣などの文字を書いたものもあった。五色の紙の持つ意味は不明とされる。 伊賀地方の祇園祭で行われる「花奪い」と類似する民俗行事で、もとは能牟良神社（のむらじんじゃ）の祭礼であり、1908年（明治41年）に忍山神社が能牟良神社を合祀したことで忍山神社の祭礼となった{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/jirei/bunrui10/data10-4/index10_4_3.htm |title=亀山市史 民俗編 祭礼・芸能（四）由来 |publisher =亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}。能牟良神社は古くは牛頭天王社と呼ばれ、忍山神社に残る棟札によると、傘鉾は天王信仰に関わる行事で江戸時代に中絶していたが、1717年（亨保13年）に再興された。 21世紀前半には例祭日に近い日曜日に日程を移動して催行されている。 == 交通アクセス == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[関西本線]][[亀山駅 (三重県)|亀山駅]]から徒歩25分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年 * 亀山市教育委員会『ヤマトタケルとオトタチバナヒメ物語』亀山市教育委員会、2023年 * {{Cite book|和書 |title=鈴鹿郡郷土誌 復刻版 |publisher=東天社 |year=1981 |ref = harv|isbn = 4-924599-06-9 |editor = 鈴鹿郡教育会}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oshiyama-jinja}} * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 亀山市史民俗編忍山神社 秋祭] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おしやましんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:亀山市の歴史]] [[Category:亀山市の建築物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:弟橘媛]]

[[天照皇大神]]
[[天児屋根命]] |画像=[[ファイル:Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (2).jpg|280px]] |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 51|緯度秒 = 17.19|N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 136|経度分 = 26|経度秒 = 13.28|E(東経)及びW(西経) =E |社格=[[式内社]] |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=10月14日 [[天児屋命]]（あめのこやねのみこと）など == 歴史 == === 古代 === [[ファイル:Ototachibanahime.png|thumb|170px|弟橘姫命]] (日本)|戦国時代]]の永正3年（1506年）には再び火災で神殿などを焼失した。[[江戸時代]]には忍山神社の神官である大久保但馬守の邸宅が竣工し、後に[[亀山神社 (亀山市)|亀山神社]]に移築されると、1955年（昭和30年）2月19日には「大久保神官家棟門」として亀山市指定文化財に指定されている[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市。 === 近代・現代 === =三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}。 == 境内 == === 建造物 === * 本殿 * 社殿 * 社務所 * 手水舎 * 南参道 鳥居 * 東参道 鳥居 Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|本殿 Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|社殿の内部 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|社務所 Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|南参道の鳥居 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|東参道の鳥居 Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|社叢 === 植生 === 亀山市の巨樹・古木・注目すべき木として数えられる11本のうち、以下の２本が忍山神社境内にある{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher =亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}。 |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/jirei/bunrui10/data10-4/index10_4_2.htm |title=亀山市史 民俗編 祭礼・芸能（四）傘鉾の制作 |publisher =亀山市 It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines. == Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊..."
revision.text:
'''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|ja|平重盛}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.

== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

revision.text:
'''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|ja|平重盛}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.

== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.

revision.text:
'''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|ja|平重盛}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

revision.text:
'''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|ja|平重盛}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

'''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines.

; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|ja|平重盛}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

revision.text:
'''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|ja|平重盛}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.

== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
'''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|ja|平重盛}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.

revision.text:
'''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|ja|平重盛}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.

'''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|ja|平重盛}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.

== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.

== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.

== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

'''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|ja|平重盛}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.

== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

revision.text:
'''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|ja|平重盛}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

revision.text:
'''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|ja|平重盛}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]
{{Shinto}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic sites in Kameyama]] [[Category:Legendary sites related to Yamato Takeru]] [[Category:Legendary sites related to Princess Ototachibana]] [[Category:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oshiyama Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oshiyama Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:忍山神社]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Oshiyama Shrine]][[Category:11]]

This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "-qq]]" to "-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]]"
revision.text:
'''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|ja|亀山市}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サルタヒコ|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|ヤマトタケル}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|ja|平重盛}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970年、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Croton|ja|モチノキ}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Japanese nutmeg|ja|イヌガヤ}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station|ja|亀山駅 (三重県)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|ja|関西本線}}.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]
