; Additional Deities * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikago-no-Iro|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, the wife of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, [[:en:Taira no Shigemori|Taira no Shigemori]] rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large [[:en:Ilex integra|Croton]] tree and an ancient [[:en:Cephalotaxus harringtonii|Japanese nutmeg]] tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|qq=|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from [[:en:Kameyama Station (Mie)|Kameyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]].

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:忍山神社 社殿.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nomura|qid=Q124652385}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.85482275221696|N|136.4370329885995|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q124652404]] Wikidata name: Oshiyama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]], Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of Ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.25</ref> * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikago-no-Iro|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, the wife of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, [[:en:Taira no Shigemori|Taira no Shigemori]] rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large [[:en:Ilex integra|Croton]] tree and an ancient [[:en:Cephalotaxus harringtonii|Japanese nutmeg]] tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|qq=|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from [[:en:Kameyama Station (Mie)|Kameyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]].

This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.

== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large [[:en:Ilex integra|Croton]] tree and an ancient [[:en:Cephalotaxus harringtonii|Japanese nutmeg]] tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|qq=|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from [[:en:Kameyama Station (Mie)|Kameyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]].

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qid=" to "WD="
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:忍山神社 社殿.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nomura|WD=Q124652385}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.85482275221696|N|136.4370329885995|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q124652404]] Wikidata name: Oshiyama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]], Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of Ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.25</ref> * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikago-no-Iro|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, the wife of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, [[:en:Taira no Shigemori|Taira no Shigemori]] rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuki Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large [[:en:Ilex integra|Croton]] tree and an ancient [[:en:Cephalotaxus harringtonii|Japanese nutmeg]] tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|qq=|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from [[:en:Kameyama Station (Mie)|Kameyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:忍山神社 社殿.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nomura|WD=Q124652385}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.85482275221696|N|136.4370329885995|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124652404]] Wikidata name: Oshiyama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]], Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of Ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.25</ref> * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikago-no-Iro|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, the wife of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, [[:en:Taira no Shigemori|Taira no Shigemori]] rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large [[:en:Ilex integra|Croton]] tree and an ancient [[:en:Cephalotaxus harringtonii|Japanese nutmeg]] tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from [[:en:Kameyama Station (Mie)|Kameyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]].
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikago-no-Iro|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, the wife of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, [[:en:Taira no Shigemori|Taira no Shigemori]] rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large [[:en:Ilex integra|Croton]] tree and an ancient [[:en:Cephalotaxus harringtonii|Japanese nutmeg]] tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from [[:en:Kameyama Station (Mie)|Kameyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]].

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of Ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.25</ref> * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikago-no-Iro|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, the wife of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, [[:en:Taira no Shigemori|Taira no Shigemori]] rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>

== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.

== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large [[:en:Ilex integra|Croton]] tree and an ancient [[:en:Cephalotaxus harringtonii|Japanese nutmeg]] tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from [[:en:Kameyama Station (Mie)|Kameyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:忍山神社 社殿.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nomura|WD=Q124652385}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.85482275221696|N|136.4370329885995|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124652404]] Wikidata name: Oshiyama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]], Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of Ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.25</ref> * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikago-no-Iro|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, the wife of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, [[:en:Taira no Shigemori|Taira no Shigemori]] rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large [[:en:Ilex integra|Croton]] tree and an ancient [[:en:Cephalotaxus harringtonii|Japanese nutmeg]] tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from [[:en:Kameyama Station (Mie)|Kameyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:忍山神社 社殿.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nomura|WD=Q124652385}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.85482275221696|N|136.4370329885995|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124652404]] Wikidata name: Oshiyama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]], Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of Ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.25</ref> * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikago-no-Iro|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, the wife of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, [[:en:Taira no Shigemori|Taira no Shigemori]] rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large [[:en:Ilex integra|Croton]] tree and an ancient [[:en:Cephalotaxus harringtonii|Japanese nutmeg]] tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from [[:en:Kameyama Station (Mie)|Kameyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]].
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:忍山神社 社殿.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nomura|WD=Q124652385}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.85482275221696|N|136.4370329885995|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124652404]] Wikidata name: Oshiyama-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kameyama, Mie|Kameyama, Mie]], Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of Ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * [[:en:Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]] (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.25</ref> * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]] (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikago-no-Iro|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, the wife of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]], who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, [[:en:Taira no Shigemori|Taira no Shigemori]] rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large [[:en:Ilex integra|Croton]] tree and an ancient [[:en:Cephalotaxus harringtonii|Japanese nutmeg]] tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from [[:en:Kameyama Station (Mie)|Kameyama Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]].
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]
{{Shinto}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:忍山神社 社殿.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nomura|WD=Q124652385}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.85482275221696|N|136.4370329885995|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of Ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikago-no-Iro|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|en|Taira no Shigemori|WD=Q1184985|lt=Taira no Shigemori}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Ilex integra|en|Ilex integra|WD=Q848316|lt=Croton}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Cephalotaxus harringtonii|en|Cephalotaxus harringtonii|WD=Q653466|lt=Japanese nutmeg}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station (Mie)|en|Kameyama Station (Mie)|WD=Q5358681|lt=Kameyama Station}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|en|Kansai Main Line|WD=Q1199703|lt=Kansai Main Line}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]
{{Shinto}}
{{translated page|ja|忍山神社|version=99448161|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:忍山神社]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:忍山神社 社殿.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nomura|WD=Q124652385|1=Nomura}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.85482275221696|N|136.4370329885995|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie|1=Kameyama, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of Ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikago-no-Iro|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|en|Taira no Shigemori|WD=Q1184985|lt=Taira no Shigemori|1=Taira no Shigemori}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Ilex integra|en|Ilex integra|WD=Q848316|lt=Croton|1=Ilex integra}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Cephalotaxus harringtonii|en|Cephalotaxus harringtonii|WD=Q653466|lt=Japanese nutmeg|1=Cephalotaxus harringtonii}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station (Mie)|en|Kameyama Station (Mie)|WD=Q5358681|lt=Kameyama Station|1=Kameyama Station (Mie)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|en|Kansai Main Line|WD=Q1199703|lt=Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai Main Line}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]
{{Shinto}}
{{translated page|ja|忍山神社|version=99448161|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:忍山神社]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Oshiyama-jinja]][[ja:忍山神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:忍山神社 社殿.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nomura|WD=Q124652385|1=Nomura}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.85482275221696|N|136.4370329885995|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie|1=Kameyama, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of Ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikago-no-Iro|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|en|Taira no Shigemori|WD=Q1184985|lt=Taira no Shigemori|1=Taira no Shigemori}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Ilex integra|en|Ilex integra|WD=Q848316|lt=Croton|1=Ilex integra}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Cephalotaxus harringtonii|en|Cephalotaxus harringtonii|WD=Q653466|lt=Japanese nutmeg|1=Cephalotaxus harringtonii}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station (Mie)|en|Kameyama Station (Mie)|WD=Q5358681|lt=Kameyama Station|1=Kameyama Station (Mie)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|en|Kansai Main Line|WD=Q1199703|lt=Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai Main Line}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * {{Official|https://www.jinja-net.jp/osiyama-jinja/}} * [http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm Kameyama City Historical Archive]
{{Shinto}}
{{translated page|ja|忍山神社|version=99448161|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:忍山神社]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|image=File:忍山神社 社殿.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nomura|WD=Q124652385|1=Nomura}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.85482275221696|N|136.4370329885995|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} '''Oshiyama Shrine''' (忍山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kameyama, Mie|en|Kameyama, Mie|WD=Q646858|lt=Kameyama, Mie|1=Kameyama, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a shrine designated under the ancient system of Ranked Shinto shrines.
== Deities Enshrined == ; Main Deities * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (猿田彦命)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.25</ref> * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} (天照大神)<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
; Additional Deities * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|en|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (天児屋命) and others<ref name="三重県郷土資料刊行会1970p25"/>
== History == === Ancient Period === According to tradition, the shrine was established in 91 BCE during the reign of Emperor Sujin, when {{ill|Ikago-no-Iro|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikago-no-Iro|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Mononobe clan, enshrined Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto upon Imperial command. The descendants of Ikago-no-Iro’s son served as the shrine priests for 80 generations until the Meiji period<ref name=県神社庁>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=63872 |title=忍山神社 |publisher=三重県神社庁、皇學館大学現代日本社会学部 |accessdate=2024-02-26}}</ref>. Additionally, {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, a daughter of Emperor Suinin, stayed at Oshiyama and enshrined Amaterasu-Ōmikami there<ref name=県神社庁/>.
The area is also said to be the birthplace of {{ill|Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Tachibanahime-no-Mikoto|lt=Princess Ototachibana|ja|弟橘媛|Ototachibana-hime-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ototachibana|qq=}}, the wife of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, who accompanied him on his eastern expedition and sacrificed herself to calm the sea gods’ wrath<ref name=県神社庁/>.
=== Medieval and Early Modern Periods === During the Heian period, {{ill|Taira no Shigemori|en|Taira no Shigemori|WD=Q1184985|lt=Taira no Shigemori|1=Taira no Shigemori}} rebuilt the shrine, and it thrived. However, the shrine was destroyed during the Mongol invasions in 1274. The deity was temporarily relocated to the grounds of {{ill|Fuki Shrine|ja|布気神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} but eventually replaced the latter as the main shrine<ref name="亀山地方">『亀山地方郷土史 第1巻』三重県郷土資料刊行会、1970th year 、p.178</ref>.
The shrine suffered further destruction in the Muromachi and Sengoku periods. During the Edo period, the residence of the shrine priests, the Ōkubo family, was constructed and later moved to Kameyama Shrine. This structure was designated a Kameyama City Cultural Property in 1955<ref name="亀山市指定文化財一覧表">[https://www.city.kameyama.mie.jp/docs/2017072000027/file_contents/shiteibunkazai.pdf 亀山市指定文化財一覧表] 亀山市</ref>.
=== Modern Era === In 1908, Oshiyama Shrine merged with 20 smaller shrines in the area. It celebrated its 2000th anniversary in 1917<ref name="パンフレット">パンフレット「忍山神社」</ref>.
== Grounds == === Structures === * Main Hall (Honden) * Worship Hall (Haiden) * Shrine Office * Ablution Pavilion (Chōzuya) * Southern Torii Gate * Eastern Torii Gate <gallery> Oshiyama-jinja honden ac.jpg|Main Hall Oshiyama-jinja shaden ac (4).jpg|Interior of the Worship Hall 忍山神社 社殿.jpg|Shrine Office Oshiyama-jinja torii ac (1).jpg|Southern Torii Gate 忍山神社 鳥居.jpg|Eastern Torii Gate Oshiyama-jinja keidai ac (3).jpg|Sacred Grove </gallery>
=== Flora === The shrine grounds include two notable trees: a large {{ill|Ilex integra|en|Ilex integra|WD=Q848316|lt=Croton|1=Ilex integra}} tree and an ancient {{ill|Cephalotaxus harringtonii|en|Cephalotaxus harringtonii|WD=Q653466|lt=Japanese nutmeg|1=Cephalotaxus harringtonii}} tree, both recognized as significant trees by Kameyama City<ref name=亀山市史自然・植物>{{Cite web |url=http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/SizenHP/plant/pla09/pla09-4.htm |title=亀山市史 自然・植物（名木・巨木） |publisher=亀山市 |accessdate=2024-02-29}}</ref>.
== Festivals == The annual {{ill|Autumn Festival|ja|秋祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Festival|1=Autumn Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11595783}} on October 14 includes the "Kasaboko Parade," designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property by Kameyama City in 1952. This parade features large umbrella floats accompanied by drumming as they process through the town<ref name="忍山神社秋祭">[http://kameyamarekihaku.jp/sisi/MinzokuHP/Video4/video4.htm 忍山神社秋祭] 亀山市</ref>.
== Access == * A 25-minute walk from {{ill|Kameyama Station (Mie)|en|Kameyama Station (Mie)|WD=Q5358681|lt=Kameyama Station|1=Kameyama Station (Mie)}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|en|Kansai Main Line|WD=Q1199703|lt=Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai Main Line}}.
